response.justification: Strong senior IC with broad full-stack and Linux/open-source experience (GISCollective, DLang, Node.js) and a clear interest in OSS — a good cultural and technical fit on paper. However, the role demands executive-level leadership of an interdisciplinary team and German at C1, and the resume shows only basic German and limited formal people-management or Head-of-Development experience. Worth a screening call to probe leadership scope and language progression.
strengths: Deep open-source and Linux/Unix background, including founding and running GISCollective
Broad senior engineering experience across architecture, full-stack, and embedded systems
Strong mentoring/teaching track record via workshops, dojos, and Selenium courses
concerns: German listed as Basic — far below the required C1 proficiency
No explicit Head-of-Development or formal team-lead title with reports and P&L-style oversight
Limited evidence of UML modeling, requirements management, and formal IT security/compliance work

key_factors.factor: German C1 requirement vs. Basic level
Lack of formal Head-of-Development leadership track record
